prompt: nul-lifestyle-1x1-08-v2-redo3: Square 1:1 cinematic tennis action photograph at a hardcourt night match. Tight framing on a male tennis player mid-serve-strike — racquet smashing upward and forward overhead at the moment of contact with a tossed yellow tennis ball, body extended upward. Wearing a navy-teal performance polo and bright orange wristband on the racquet arm. The racquet itself is rendered clearly recognizable and CRISP at the strike point — head, strings, and frame all readable, hand gripping the handle. Slow-shutter motion blur trails the racquet as a CONTINUOUS translucent SMEAR — a STRAIGHT, linear directional ribbon-streak extending behind the racquet along the strike path. The smear must be linear and follow the direct line of the swing — NOT a curved arc, NOT a J-shape, NOT bending sideways. Imagine a single long-exposure frame where the racquet has traced one straight motion-line from where it was a fraction of a second earlier to where it is now at impact. The striking arm and forearm also smear into a continuous straight directional trail along the same line. The player's body, face, polo, and the rest of his frame remain tack-sharp. Dim moody arena lighting, deep navy court / blue-black background. Subtle fine 35mm cinematic grain — understated, not heavy. Rich saturated shadows. Editorial sports photography, no text, no logos.
